assembly_commands.name: ledger-empty-root
assembly_commands.meaning: Merkle Tree Hash for an empty WUCI-LEDGER

assembly_commands.name: ledger-leaf-file
assembly_commands.meaning: Domain-separated WUCI-LEDGER leaf hash for exact entry file bytes

assembly_commands.name: ledger-node
assembly_commands.input: left and right are 64-hex SHA-256 child commitments

python_commands.name: init
python_commands.meaning: Initialize an empty append-only WUCI-LEDGER with tree-size 0

python_commands.name: append
python_commands.meaning: Verify a public WUCI-WITNESS bundle, derive a canonical ledger entry, append it, and write the next ledger head

python_commands.name: prove-inclusion
python_commands.meaning: Emit a Merkle inclusion proof for a sequence under the current ledger head

python_commands.name: verify-inclusion
python_commands.meaning: Verify that a canonical ledger entry is included under a ledger head

python_commands.name: prove-consistency
python_commands.meaning: Emit an append-only consistency proof between two ledger heads

python_commands.name: verify-consistency
python_commands.meaning: Verify that a later ledger head extends an earlier ledger head

format_constraints: Ledger entries are fixed-order label/value lines.
Leaf hashing uses exact entry file bytes, including the final newline.
Internal node inputs are decoded 32-byte hashes, not ASCII hex bytes.
Assembly owns only the hash commitment primitives at this stage.
Python owns append, inclusion proof, and consistency proof orchestration for the first full ledger step.
Ledger append must verify the public WUCI-WITNESS bundle before deriving a leaf.
Inclusion and consistency proof hashing must use the assembly ledger primitives.
non_goals: Do not parse publish attestations in assembly in this first ledger step.
Do not accept arbitrary signer material.
Do not replace WUCI-PUBLISH or WUCI-WITNESS verification.
Do not add new secret-bearing crypto.
